# Title: Account Health Assurance: Frequently asked questions

Title: Account Health Assurance: Frequently asked questions

URL Source: https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/GGQY933W4RDPJT29

Markdown Content:
#### What is Account Health Assurance?

Account Health Assurance (AHA) is a benefit available to professional sellers who consistently achieve a high Account Health Rating (AHR). With Account Health Assurance, we will no longer deactivate your selling account as long as you work with us within 72 hours of contacting you to resolve any issues.

#### I already maintain great account health and work with support. What is different about program?

AHA provides an extra layer of protection. If you encounter an issue that could result in an account deactivation, we will keep your account active, and you will be able to continue selling while we work together to promptly resolve the problem. Your excellent account health qualifies you for this additional security.

#### Do I qualify for AHA?

#### Do I qualify for AHA?

AHA is available to professional sellers who maintain an Account Health Rating (AHR) score of 250 or higher for at least 6 months with no more than 10 days below 250 and have a valid emergency contact number on file. You will receive an email confirming your enrollment as soon as you’re eligible, and you can check your progress toward becoming eligible [eligibility page](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/ah/eligibility).

#### If I qualify, is my account automatically in the program?

Yes. You will be notified of your enrollment via email. You can also see your enrollment status on your Account Health page. If you meet the Account Health Rating (AHR) requirements, but you do not have an emergency contact number, we will notify you via email that you are eligible and ask you to provide emergency contact information before you are enrolled. Ensure that your emergency contact number is always accurate and up to date so we can reach you promptly when necessary.

#### Where is AHA available?

AHA is available to professional sellers who qualify for the program in Amazon stores. We look forward to expanding the program to additional stores around the world as they become available.

#### If I don’t qualify now for AHA, can I qualify and be enrolled later?

Yes. We continuously review accounts and enroll sellers in AHA as soon as they qualify. You’ll receive both an email notification and an alert on your Account Health page upon enrollment. If you haven’t been enrolled in AHA, refer to FAQ 3 for guidance on how to become eligible or check your progress on the[eligibility page](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/ah/eligibility).

#### I don’t qualify for AHA, but I need help. How do I get help?

If you do not qualify for AHA, you can still reach the Account Health Support team at any time for help related to account health issues by using "Call Me Now" on your Account Health page.

#### I do not qualify for AHA, but I need help with my account health. How do I get help?

If you do not qualify for AHA and are a professional seller, you can still reach the Account Health Support team at any time for help related to account health issues. To contact Account Health Support, click **Contact us/Call me now** on your Account Health page <ph country="US">or use Amazon's Seller Assistant</ph>.

If you are not a professional seller, [Seller University](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/learn) and [Seller Forums](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/forums) are resources available to all Amazon sellers.

#### Can I lose access to AHA?

To continue to be eligible for AHA, you must meet the following requirements:

#### Can I lose access to AHA?

To continue to be eligible for AHA, you must meet the following requirements:

*   Keep your AHR from falling below 250 for more than 10 consecutive days
*   Maintain a verified emergency contact number in Seller Central
*   Meet all regulatory compliance obligations in the Amazon stores where you operate
*   Engage with our account health team within 72 hours of notification that we need to work with you regarding an issue
*   Fully resolve issues when working with Account Health Support
*   Avoid repeating the same policy compliance issues

Regardless of your AHA eligibility status, Amazon may remove you from the AHA program and deactivate your seller account immediately if we believe you have engaged in fraudulent, deceptive, illegal, or otherwise harmful activity, in order to protect customers, our selling partners, and Amazon’s store,– as stipulated under the [Amazon Services Business Solutions Agreement](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/gp/help/external/G201190440).

#### How can I verify my emergency contact number?

1.   Open the [Notification Preferences](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/notifications/preferences/ref=xx_notifpref_dnav_xx) page.
2.   Look for the Emergency Notification widget.
3.   Check the color-coded tag (green = verified, red = needs verification).
4.   Complete a simple one-time code verification.

#### What if I have a seasonal business and my AHR score falls below 250 due to fewer fulfilled orders after I am enrolled?

#### What if I have a seasonal business and my AHR score falls below 250 due to fewer fulfilled orders after I am enrolled?

Should your AHR drop below 250 after enrollment in the program, but you have no policy violations on your account health page, you will remain enrolled for an additional 180 days, even with an AHR below 250. However, if you incur any new policy violations during this period, you must appeal and resolve them within 10 days; otherwise, you will lose access to AHA. You can track the number of days your AHR remains below 250 on the [AHA eligibility page](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/ah/eligibility).

#### What if I cannot answer the phone when you call me about an urgent issue?

#### What if I cannot answer the phone when you call me about an urgent issue?

To resolve an issue promptly, we will need to speak to you within 72 hours. You will receive both a phone call and an email with instructions on how to get in touch with us. If you can’t answer the phone the first time we call, we will try again, or you can call our team directly using the "Call me Now" button found on your account health page. Alternatively, you can submit a plan for how you intend to resolve the issue on your account health page. But if we need more information from you, we may request that you speak to us over the phone before resolving the issue.

#### How can I reach an Account Health specialist?

You can reach an Account Health specialist at any time by using the "Call me Now" button found on your account health page.

#### My Account Health Rating hasn’t been updated to a numeric score yet. How do I know if I’m eligible for Account Health Assurance?

#### My Account Health Rating hasn’t been updated to a numeric score yet. How do I know if I’m eligible for Account Health Assurance?

If you can’t see a numeric AHR score yet, you are not eligible for Account Health Assurance.

#### Where can I learn more about the Account Health Rating?

You can learn more about the Account Health Rating on the [program policy page](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/gp/help/external/G200205250).

#### What is Seller Challenge?

Refer to the [Seller Challenge FAQ](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/gp/help/external/G7XWXBNSYMBJAPX5) page for additional details.
